focus: primary workflow completeness, reference grouping, relative path/script/flag evidence, validator baseline

findingDetails.title: Primary install-guide template contradicts the workflow's required 0-10 section contract
findingDetails.evidence: SKILL.md routes install-guide work to assets/readme/install_guide_template.md and requires sections 0 through 10, while the template exposes copyable sections 11 through 14.
findingDetails.recommendation: Make the copyable install-guide scaffold match sections 0-10 and move maintenance/best-practices/related resources to non-copyable overflow.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Direct reads covered SKILL.md primary template routing and required structure plus install_guide_template.md section headings 11-14.

findingDetails.title: Template deprecation example links to a non-existent local install guide
findingDetails.evidence: The template links to ./MCP%20-%20New%20Tool.md; relative-path resolver reported BROKEN ./MCP - New Tool.md.
findingDetails.recommendation: Replace with a placeholder href, an existing example, or an explicit note that the href must be customized.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Relative path resolver checked target docs and asset templates; this was the only non-placeholder broken concrete asset example link reported.

noveltyJustification: First initialized review iteration produced one P1 template-contract mismatch and one P2 broken concrete example link; prior registry contained no findings.

relative path resolver run against target docs and assets

Stale code graph was not needed for documentation evidence

focus: correctness and template fidelity, SKILL self-sufficiency and reference overflow boundaries

findingDetails.title: Install-guide template teaches a non-table troubleshooting format where the workflow requires a 3-column table
findingDetails.evidence: SKILL.md and the install-guide quality reference require a 3-column Error/Cause/Fix troubleshooting table, but the primary template's immediate example uses bold error headings with Cause/Fix bullets.
findingDetails.recommendation: Replace the bullet-style troubleshooting examples with the exact Error/Cause/Fix table shape, including at least five rows in the copyable scaffold or explicit instructions to add five rows.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Direct reads compared SKILL.md troubleshooting format, install-guide quality reference, and the primary install-guide template examples.

noveltyJustification: Iteration 2 adds one new P1 correctness finding on troubleshooting format. Prior active weighted total was 6; new weighted total is 5 over accumulated active total 11.

focus: security, maintainability, relative path/tool/flag/section claim verification and asset/reference coverage

findingDetails.title: API-key troubleshooting example prints secrets to the terminal
findingDetails.evidence: The install-guide template tells users to export API_KEY_VAR and then echo it to verify it is set, exposing copied real keys in terminal output, logs or AI transcripts.
findingDetails.recommendation: Replace with a non-printing check such as test -n plus a non-secret success message, and add safe secret-store/environment injection guidance.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Security grep across the target found the only API-key handling example at install_guide_template.md:344-345; direct reads found no surrounding warning offsetting printed-secret behavior.

noveltyJustification: Iteration 3 adds one new P1 security finding. Prior active weighted total was 11; new weighted total is 5 over accumulated active total 16.

findingDetails.title: Install-guide template still carries stale parent-hub mode wording
findingDetails.evidence: The template labels ../../SKILL.md as sk-doc SKILL.md - Mode 4: Install Guide Creation even though the current packet is a nested create-readme workflow with a local authoritative SKILL.md.
findingDetails.recommendation: Rename the related-resource label to current create-readme nested-packet wording.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Grep for Mode 4, sk-doc SKILL, legacy, deprecated and monolith found this active stale mode label as the only durable parent-mode wording outside illustrative deprecation prose.
